summary: Tested Yrvante Lead Finder backend API and frontend UI. Frontend loads correctly with all expected elements. Backend /api/zoek returns 502 because the Google Places API (New) is NOT enabled for the project associated with the provided API key.

critical.issue: Returns 502. Google Places API (New) is disabled for project 1080002902278. Error: 'Places API (New) has not been used in project 1080002902278 before or it is disabled.' Must enable at: https://console.developers.google.com/apis/api/places.googleapis.com/overview?project=1080002902278

integration_issues.issue: All searches fail with 'Google Places API fout: 403' error banner because the backend Google Places API key is not activated. Error state UI works correctly.

action_items: CRITICAL: Enable 'Places API (New)' in Google Cloud Console for project 1080002902278 at https://console.developers.google.com/apis/api/places.googleapis.com/overview?project=1080002902278 OR provide a valid API key that already has Places API (New) enabled.
Alternatively, check if a different API key should be used - the current key AIzaSyDn1y6-aOzY8NXaTdJAPL0GqI_5rl5XMEM is valid but the API is not enabled for its project.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend code is well-structured, filtering logic is correct (websiteUri check on line 101), response model is correct.
Frontend UI is complete with proper data-testid attributes, loading skeleton, error/empty states, CSV export.
Loading skeleton was not visible during test (likely too brief) - may want to add a minimum loading display time.

success_rate.backend: 40% (4/10 tests pass - health/validation pass; all real API calls fail due to Google API not enabled)
success_rate.frontend: 80% (UI loads, form works, error state works; results/CSV/loading states untestable due to API failure)

context_for_next_testing_agent: The only blocker is the Google Places API (New) not being enabled for project 1080002902278. Once that is fixed, retest POST /api/zoek for proper response structure, filtering (no websiteUri in returned leads, totaal_gevonden >= leads count), and frontend results table, CSV export, loading skeleton.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: Google Places API key AIzaSyDn1y6-aOzY8NXaTdJAPL0GqI_5rl5XMEM is valid but belongs to GCP project 1080002902278 where 'Places API (New)' (places.googleapis.com) has never been enabled. The backend uses the new Places API v1 endpoint (places.googleapis.com/v1/places:searchText) which requires this specific API to be enabled. Fix: Go to https://console.developers.google.com/apis/api/places.googleapis.com/overview?project=1080002902278 and click Enable.
